title: podman v4.0.2: play kube command ignores hostNetwork directive in pod description
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** In podman v4.0.2 `podman play kube` command does not respect the hostNetwork parameter in a pod spec. On the other hand, podman v3.4.7 works as expected. Similar issue for older 3.x releases has been fixed ~2 years ago: see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/4432 **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Create a pod description that uses the hostNetwork parameter: Use simple httpd container to check accessibility from host: ``` $ cat test1.yaml apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: creationTimestamp: "2022-04-26T15:06:25Z" labels: app: mypod name: mypod spec: restartPolicy: Never hostNetwork: true containers: - image: docker.io/library/httpd:latest name: blissfulpayne resources: {} securityContext: capabilities: drop: - CAP_MKNOD - CAP_NET_RAW - CAP_AUDIT_WRITE tty: true ``` 3. Check container using podman v4.0.2 ``` $ podman --version podman version 4.0.2 $ podman play kube test1.yaml $ wget localhost:80 -O - Connecting to localhost:80 (127.0.0.1:80) wget: can't connect to remote host (127.0.0.1): Connection refused $ podman pod rm mypod -f ``` 3. Check container using podman v3.4.7 ``` $ ./podman --version podman version 3.4.7 $ ./podman play kube test1.yaml $ wget localhost:80 -O - Connecting to localhost:80 (127.0.0.1:80) writing to stdout

It works!

- 100% |******************************************************************************************************************************************************************************************************************************************| 45 0:00:00 ETA written to stdout $ ./podman pod rm mypod -f ``` **Describe the results you received:** See above. **Describe the results you expected:** I would expect the container to run in the host network namespace. This is what I get when I am using podman v3.4.7. However the result is not the same when I am using podman v4.0.2. **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** N/A **Output of `podman version`:** For podman v4.0.2: ``` $ podman version Client: Podman Engine Version: 4.0.2 API Version: 4.0.2 Go Version: go1.17.8 Built: Thu Jan 1 00:00:00 1970 OS/Arch: linux/arm ``` For podman v3.4.7: ``` $ ./podman version Version: 3.4.7 API Version: 3.4.7 Go Version: go1.17.8 Built: Thu Jan 1 00:00:00 1970 OS/Arch: linux/arm ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** For podman v4.0.2: ``` host: arch: arm buildahVersion: 1.24.1 cgroupControllers: - cpuset - cpu - cpuacct - blkio - memory - devices - freezer - net_cls - perf_event - net_prio - pids cgroupManager: cgroupfs cgroupVersion: v1 conmon: package: Unknown path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.29, commit: 96b346cb56ccf5ef52b29d367db5149d212c45df' cpus: 4 distribution: distribution: buildroot version: "2022.02" eventLogger: file hostname: OrangePiPCPlus idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 4.14.253 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: k8s-file memFree: 722657280 memTotal: 1051852800 networkBackend: cni ociRuntime: name: crun package: Unknown path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.4.3 commit: 61c9600d1335127eba65632731e2d72bc3f0b9e8 spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: false seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: "" selinuxEnabled: false serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: "" package: "" version: "" swapFree: 0 swapTotal: 0 uptime: 4h 6m 48.16s (Approximately 0.17 days) plugins: log: - k8s-file - none - passthrough network: - bridge - macvlan - ipvlan volume: - local registries: search: - docker.io - registry.fedoraproject.org - registry.access.redhat.com - registry.centos.org store: configFile: /usr/share/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 0 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 5 runRoot: /run/containers/storage volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 4.0.2 Built: 0 BuiltTime: Thu Jan 1 00:00:00 1970 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.17.8 OsArch: linux/arm Version: 4.0.2 ``` For podman v3.4.7: ``` $ ./podman info --debug host: arch: arm buildahVersion: 1.23.1 cgroupControllers: - cpuset - cpu - cpuacct - blkio - memory - devices - freezer - net_cls - perf_event - net_prio - pids cgroupManager: cgroupfs cgroupVersion: v1 conmon: package: Unknown path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.29, commit: 96b346cb56ccf5ef52b29d367db5149d212c45df' cpus: 4 distribution: distribution: buildroot version: "2022.02" eventLogger: file hostname: OrangePiPCPlus idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 4.14.253 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: k8s-file memFree: 724201472 memTotal: 1051852800 ociRuntime: name: crun package: Unknown path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.4.3 commit: 61c9600d1335127eba65632731e2d72bc3f0b9e8 spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: false seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: "" selinuxEnabled: false serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: "" package: "" version: "" swapFree: 0 swapTotal: 0 uptime: 4h 7m 58.71s (Approximately 0.17 days) plugins: log: - k8s-file - none network: - bridge - macvlan volume: - local registries: search: - docker.io - registry.fedoraproject.org - registry.access.redhat.com - registry.centos.org store: configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 0 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageStore: number: 5 runRoot: /run/containers/storage volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 3.4.7 Built: 0 BuiltTime: Thu Jan 1 00:00:00 1970 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.17.8 OsArch: linux/arm Version: 3.4.7 ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** N/A: cross-compiled podman for ARMv7 platform **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)** Yes **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** N/A

comments.body:
Steps to reproduce the issue:
```
podman run -d --name t1 --net host docker.io/library/nginx:alpine
podman generate kube t1 > test.yml
podman rm -f t1
podman play kube test.yml
$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND               CREATED        STATUS            PORTS       NAMES
ecf38bf932ce  localhost/podman-pause:4.0.3-1648835798                        9 seconds ago  Up 4 seconds ago              6ce005998dcc-infra
afa8d4c24481  docker.io/library/nginx:alpine           nginx -g daemon o...  4 seconds ago  Up 3 seconds ago              t1_pod-t1
$ ip r g 1
1.0.0.0 via 192.168.2.3 dev eth0 src 192.168.2.121 uid 0 
    cache 
$ podman exec t1_pod-t1 ip a s 
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: eth0@if4: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP 
    link/ether 22:91:12:64:0f:fb brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.88.0.2/16 brd 10.88.255.255 scope global eth0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::2091:12ff:fe64:ffb/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
```
